name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical development, CMC support, and process development for small molecule and nucleotide chemistry. Expertise in method development using HPLC, FT-IR, UV-Vis, and mass spectrometry (LC-MS, GC-MS), as well as thermal analysis (TGA, DSC). Strong background in statistical analysis (DOE, SPC), method validation, and supporting manufacturing processes. Proven track record of managing multiple projects and delivering high-quality analytical data.

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

bullets: Develop analytical methods and apply statistical process control to support process development and scale-up.
Characterize small molecules and polymers using HPLC, FT-IR, and thermal analysis.
Developed and validated analytical methods using HPLC, FT-IR, UV-Vis, LC-MS, and GC-MS for compound characterization.
Applied DOE methodologies to optimize synthetic processes and analytical methods.
Performed thermal analysis (TGA, DSC) to assess stability and degradation behavior.

skills.Analytical Methods: HPLC, LC-MS, GC-MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, NMR
skills.CMC & Process: CMC analytical development, method validation, process development support, DOE, SPC

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.
